extraction.one_line_summary: BILBAO Pello won from the breakaway-turned-GC group sprint after LOPEZ Miguel Angel crashed from crowd interference on the descent, CARAPAZ Richard securing overall victory.
tactical_patterns: Lopez attacked twice on Croce d'Aune but crashed descending from crowd interference, eliminating strongest climber from GC podium fight.
Carapaz led out Landa for stage win attempt despite holding GC lead, revealing Movistar prioritized stage over preserving leader energy.
Roglic rode tempo for entire final climb defending 3rd place, cracked completely losing 54 seconds showing TTT domestique role depleted reserves.

rider_signals.LOPEZ Miguel Angel: negative: attacked strongly on Croce d'Aune but crashed descending from crowd interference, fifth mechanical/incident of race revealing fragility under pressure
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: led out teammate Landa for sprint despite defending GC, showing exceptional confidence in 1:54 advantage with ITT remaining
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: positive: nearly won stage after massive day, sealed podium with time gaps showing peak form arrives late in Grand Tours
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: negative: rode entire final climb defending third place but cracked losing 54 seconds, domestique work earlier in race left insufficient reserves
rider_signals.BILBAO Pello: positive: took Carapaz's wheel knowing leadout was for Landa, tactical awareness to exploit teamwork dynamics for personal stage win
